prompt: Using the provided reference image as a layout and style guide, recreate it as a vintage Chinese mythological illustrated story page instead of the Monkey King page. Keep the aged textbook/comic print look, yellowed paper texture, retro ink-and-watercolor rendering, and boxed multi-panel composition. Replace the original content with a 山海经-themed page. Preserve the vertical title block on the left, but change the main title to 山海经. Build a total of 8 content panels: 1 large title/hero panel at the top left featuring a mythic figure and a red sun, 5 medium boxed narrative panels in the middle rows with different ancient myth scenes, and 2 wide horizontal panels across the bottom row. Add dense Chinese explanatory captions under each panel in the same old-print style, with small numbered labels for panels 2 through 8 and a small red seal-like tag near the title. The overall result should feel like an antique Chinese serialized picture-book page from a retro children’s mythology encyclopedia.
